title: Theories and modules: creation myths, developmental realities, and Neurath's boat
abstract: I and others have made a case for the similarities between scientific knowledge and children's knowledge, and between scientific change and cognitive development (Gopnik and Wellman, 1992, 1994; Gopnik, 1988; Carey, 1986, 1988; Keil, 1989; Wellman, 1990). In the rest of this chapter I will call this general view of cognitive development the 'theory-formation theory'.
